summary: A 3D matrix-column intuition scene. A true ThreeDScene stage uses neutral gray ticked axes, translucent coordinate planes, fixed-orientation labels, and an updater that rotates the shifted 3D stage about its own local Z-axis. It shows the standard basis vectors, transforms copies of them to their landing vectors, then a fixed right panel records each landing point as a column and copies those columns into the final 3x3 matrix.
